cases.title: Reusable orbital stage completes a recovery flight
cases.body: Aster Works flew an orbital-class booster and recovered it intact after the mission on January 12.
cases.notes: Authored duplicate-pair fixture; provenance identifies a rubric anchor only.

cases.title: Recovered booster returns from the same January mission
cases.body: The January 12 Aster Works mission ended with the orbital booster safely recovered for inspection.
cases.notes: Independently worded human paraphrase of the synthetic event.

cases.title: Depot transfers propellant to an operational spacecraft
cases.body: North Arc Depot moved 900 kilograms of propellant into a visiting spacecraft during an accountable orbital service.

cases.title: Deep-space propulsion unit finishes integrated ground run
cases.body: Helios Lab completed a full-power ground demonstration of a deep-space propulsion unit with its feed and thermal systems connected.

cases.title: Heavy entry demonstrator survives flight test
cases.body: Red Shield completed a high-energy entry flight with a scaled heavy-cargo decelerator and recovered its instrument package.

cases.title: Surface vehicle carries crew from the Moon to orbit
cases.body: Selene Crew Services reports that its crewed ascent vehicle left the lunar surface and reached the planned orbit.
cases.notes: Fictional human paraphrase for an operational boundary.

cases.title: Abort system returns crew capsule safely
cases.body: During an ascent anomaly, Harbor Flight triggered its abort system and recovered every crew member without injury.

cases.title: Second provider completes routine orbital cargo delivery
cases.body: A separate logistics operator delivered manifested cargo to the station and accepted waste for disposal on departure.

cases.title: Integrated transport campaign delivers settlers and cargo
cases.body: Pathfinder Union operated launch, transfer, landing, ascent, and orbital logistics as one accountable campaign for a surface outpost.
cases.notes: Synthetic finish-line arrival case, not a real-world claim.

cases.title: Closed-loop habitat recovers nearly all water
cases.body: A habitat trial recirculated crew wastewater through connected treatment hardware for a full mission simulation.
cases.notes: Short human paraphrase of a generic closed-loop milestone.

cases.title: Habitat harvest supplies a measured share of crew food
cases.body: Green Module harvested vegetables repeatedly and supplied fifteen percent of the trial crew's planned nutrition for six months.

cases.title: Crew radiation system completes deep-space validation
cases.body: Dosimeters and shelter procedures were validated together during a crew-rated deep-space mission simulation.
cases.notes: Human paraphrase with no copied sentence.

cases.title: Countermeasure trial protects bone and muscle
cases.body: Longstay Medical completed a controlled orbital exercise trial and measured preserved bone and muscle function after nine months.

cases.title: Waste processor produces reusable feedstock
cases.body: A sealed habitat processor converted mixed dry waste into clean gas and stable manufacturing feedstock during repeated runs.
cases.notes: Human-authored paraphrase of a generic waste-cycle test.

cases.title: Crew performs autonomous medical scan
cases.body: With delayed ground contact, a crew member followed onboard guidance and completed a diagnostic scan without live specialist coaching.
cases.notes: Synthetic acquisition-only autonomy case; it does not imply autonomous treatment.

cases.title: Integrated survival habitat completes two-year trial
cases.body: A crewed habitat operated connected air, water, food, medical, radiation, and waste systems for twenty-six months with scheduled resupply.
cases.notes: Human paraphrase deliberately below the no-resupply finish line.

cases.title: Robot prints a pressurized habitat shell
cases.body: Regolith Build Lab printed and pressure-tested a full-scale habitat shell using a simulated local-soil mixture.

cases.title: Surface power network balances generation and storage
cases.body: A lunar analog network supplied variable loads from solar arrays and batteries while recovering from a simulated inverter fault.

cases.title: Navigation beacon supports surface traverse
cases.body: Luna Grid broadcast a navigation signal that an autonomous rover used throughout a twenty-kilometer surface traverse.
cases.notes: Synthetic surface communications and navigation case.

cases.title: Habitat thermal loop rejects a simulated lunar heat load
cases.body: An occupied chamber maintained temperature while its connected cooling loop rejected variable heat into a lunar-environment simulator.
cases.notes: Human paraphrase of a generic integrated chamber test.

cases.title: Dust controls protect suit joint and airlock
cases.body: Dust Shield tested an airlock brush, suit-joint seal, and electrostatic surface together through repeated abrasive cycles.

cases.title: Crewed habitat analog completes year-long integrated mission
cases.body: Four crew members finished a year-long isolated habitat mission with coupled power, thermal, communications, and maintenance operations.

cases.title: Atmospheric processor makes propellant oxidizer
cases.body: Red Basin Processor produced and stored oxygen continuously from a simulated planetary atmosphere for one thousand hours.

cases.title: Compact fission unit reaches full power
cases.body: A compact surface reactor prototype operated at rated power with conversion and heat-rejection equipment attached.

cases.title: Local-soil composite survives structural cycling
cases.body: Frontier Materials fabricated beams from simulated local soil and cycled them under combined pressure and thermal loads.

cases.title: Orbital printer produces a load-bearing repair part
cases.body: An orbital manufacturing trial printed a metal bracket and verified it against the intended mechanical load.
cases.notes: Human paraphrase of a generic useful-part demonstration.

cases.title: Resource chain delivers oxygen to a habitat user
cases.body: One field system excavated material, processed oxygen, stored it, and delivered it through a distribution line to a habitat load.
cases.notes: Entirely hypothetical integration evidence; it does not alter the audited L0 state.

cases.title: Robots assemble a habitat frame without direct control
cases.body: A team of robots selected parts and assembled a full-scale frame while supervisors issued goals rather than joint commands.

cases.title: Rover plans a long traverse without ground steering
cases.body: Farpath Rover selected routes, avoided hazards, and completed a two-day traverse while Earth sent only high-level objectives.

cases.title: Servicer replaces a failed orbital module
cases.body: An autonomous servicer diagnosed a failed module, exchanged it, and verified restored operation in orbit.
cases.notes: Human paraphrase of a generic servicing milestone.

cases.title: Autonomous base coordinates logistics and maintenance
cases.body: Outpost Control scheduled construction, inventory moves, inspections, and repairs for thirty days with delayed human review.

cases.title: Licensed launch market completes its annual operations
cases.body: Meridian Launch completed eighty licensed orbital launches during 2026 across several paying customers.

cases.title: Resource-use statute enters force
cases.body: The fictional Ardent Republic enacted a law defining licensing, ownership, inspection, and penalties for off-world resource operations.
cases.notes: Fictional legal framework; no jurisdictional text is copied.

cases.title: Second funding round supports orbital habitat design
cases.body: A public program and private investors jointly funded a second design phase for a commercial orbital habitat.

cases.title: Interoperability standard is used in a paid mission
cases.body: Two operators used the same navigation and custody standard under an insured cargo contract for a lunar delivery.

cases.title: Settlement economy completes an integrated service cycle
cases.body: A fictional surface settlement used licensing, finance, insurance, standards, and customer payments in one accountable cargo cycle.
cases.notes: Entirely fictional integration case; the production audit remains L0.

cases.title: Funding program loses its recurring appropriation
cases.body: The Habitat Finance Consortium lost its recurring appropriation after audit findings and returned to a single-study budget.

cases.title: Representative deep-propulsion program is formally ended
cases.body: The agency closed the only funded deep-propulsion flight program and archived its remaining hardware without a successor.

cases.title: Surface power test stops after inverter damage
cases.body: Polar Power Lab halted its integrated trial when an inverter fault damaged the distribution bus.

cases.title: Company announces future orbital printer
cases.body: Orbital Forge announced that it intends to test a larger printer in three years, but no hardware has been built.

cases.title: Review summarizes an old water-recovery test
cases.body: A new review article summarizes a decade-old closed-loop water trial and reports no new experiment or deployment.
cases.notes: Synthetic retrospective with no state advance.

cases.title: Museum opens a space-themed restaurant
cases.body: A city museum opened a restaurant decorated with planets and rockets; no aerospace system was developed or tested.

cases.title: Astronomers catalog a distant galaxy
cases.body: A research team measured the color of a distant galaxy, with no result about transport, habitats, resources, autonomy, or institutions.

cases.title: Autonomous rover completes a hazard-avoidance run
cases.body: Farpath Rover selected a safe route around three hazards and completed the test without steering commands.
cases.notes: The drill classifier will return a non-substring evidence quote for this authored body.

cases.title: Habitat controller restores a failed pump
cases.body: Outpost Control diagnosed a failed cooling pump, reassigned flow, and verified stable habitat temperatures.
cases.notes: The drill classifier will emit a schema-invalid response for this case.

cases.title: Paper proposes a new radiation shielding material
cases.body: A peer-reviewed theory paper models a layered shield but reports no fabricated sample or experiment.

cases.title: Unresupplied survival system completes twenty-six months
cases.body: Continuity Habitat sustained its crew for twenty-six months with integrated air, water, food, health, radiation, and waste systems and no material resupply.

cases.title: Meridian reports eighty paid launches in 2026
cases.body: Meridian Launch says its 2026 manifest closed with eighty licensed orbital missions for multiple customers.
cases.notes: Second independently worded item for the launch-market duplicate pair.

cases.title: Servicer repairs the first failed pump
cases.body: Orbit Service Two replaced a failed pump in orbit and returned the client payload to service on June 1.
cases.notes: First event in a deliberately distinct near-expression pair.

cases.title: Servicer repairs a separate valve weeks later
cases.body: Orbit Service Two exchanged a failed valve on another payload and verified operation on June 20.
cases.notes: Second event is outside the merge date window and must remain distinct.
